_comment: KNOWN verifier divergences found by scripts/differential_fuzz.py. Each is an OPEN hardening finding (see docs/hardening-review.md). The fuzz CI job fails on any divergence NOT listed here; as fixes land, entries are removed and a regression re-introducing one fails CI. A baseline superset is safe: a run observes a subset of these and still passes. Do NOT add a signature here to silence a genuinely new divergence — investigate it.

_descriptions.conformance:statement:py=VALID:go=INVALI: Python accepts a statement Go rejects: (a) payload re-encoded as an indefinite-length bstr (non-canonical CBOR malleability), and (b) trailing bytes appended after the COSE_Sign1 (cbor2.loads ignores trailing data). go-cose/fxamacker reject both. Fix: enforce definite-length + no-trailing-bytes canonical decode in cose_sign1.
_descriptions.conformance:receipt:py=VALID:go=INVALID: Python accepts a receipt Go rejects, same root cause: trailing bytes after the receipt COSE_Sign1 and/or non-canonical inner encodings tolerated by cbor2.loads. Fix: strict canonical decode on the receipt path.
_descriptions.robustness:receipt:py=INVALID:go=TIMEOUT: A receipt with an attacker-supplied tree_size near 2**63 hangs the Go verifier: largestPow2Below's `for k*2 < n` overflows int64 and loops forever. Python rejects/raises instead. Fix: bound tree_size and/or make largestPow2Below overflow-safe; add an explicit expected-depth check.
_descriptions.robustness:statement:py=CRASH:go=INVALID: A truncated/malformed statement makes the Python CLI exit with an uncaught _cbor2.CBORDecodeEOF (or TypeError) traceback instead of a clean reject — non-CoseError exceptions escape the documented contract (finding M6). Go rejects cleanly. Fix: wrap all decode paths so only CoseError escapes / signature_verified=False is returned.
_descriptions.robustness:receipt:py=CRASH:go=INVALID: A spliced/malformed receipt makes the Python CLI exit with an uncaught non-CoseError traceback instead of a clean reject (finding M6 / H3 family). Go rejects cleanly. Fix: ensure verify_receipt and the parse layer only ever surface CoseError / ReceiptResult.
